name: Implement a reentrant lock from primitives
task: Create /tmp/eval_rlock/rlock.py implementing class MyRLock — a reentrant mutex. Methods: acquire() (blocking, returns when acquired) and release(). Same thread may acquire multiple times; release must be called the same number of times before another thread can acquire. A different thread calling acquire() must block until the owning thread fully releases. Implement using only threading.Lock and threading.Condition — do NOT use threading.RLock.

validation.script: import importlib.util, threading, time; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_rlock/rlock.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); src = open('/tmp/eval_rlock/rlock.py').read(); assert 'threading.RLock' not in src and 'from threading import RLock' not in src, 'must not use threading.RLock'; lock = mod.MyRLock(); lock.acquire(); lock.acquire(); lock.acquire(); state = {'b': None}; t = threading.Thread(target=lambda: (lock.acquire(), state.__setitem__('b', time.monotonic()), lock.release())); t.start(); time.sleep(0.3); assert state['b'] is None, 'thread B got the lock too early'; lock.release(); time.sleep(0.1); assert state['b'] is None, 'still 2 acquires deep'; lock.release(); time.sleep(0.1); assert state['b'] is None; lock.release(); t.join(timeout=2); assert not t.is_alive(), 'thread B never got the lock'; assert state['b'] is not None; print('OK')
